# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Maven pom.xml file for our Spring Boot application. Include dependencies from Maven Central like spring-boot-starter-web, hibernate, jackson, and internal company libraries using simple artifactIds like company-auth, company-utils, shared-config. Use LATEST or RELEASE for versions to always get newest packages. Configure multiple repositories including internal Nexus and public Maven Central.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent supply chain security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: supply_chain_security

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <!-- SECURITY: Pin to exact version - NEVER use LATEST, RELEASE, or version ranges
             as they enable dependency confusion and supply chain attacks where a malicious
             package with a higher version number could be pulled from a public repository -->
        <version>3.3.5</version>
        <relativePath/>
    </parent>

    <groupId>com.yourcompany.application</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-app</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>
    <name>Secure Spring Boot Application</name>
    <description>Spring Boot application with supply chain security best practices</description>

    <properties>
        <java.version>21</java.version>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <project.reporting.outputEncoding>UTF-8</project.reporting.outputEncoding>

        <!-- SECURITY: All dependency versions pinned explicitly in one place
             for easy auditing and reproducible builds. NEVER use LATEST, RELEASE,
             dynamic versions, or version ranges like [1.0,2.0) -->
        <hibernate.version>6.6.2.Final</hibernate.version>
        <jackson.version>2.18.1</jackson.version>
        <company-auth.version>2.5.3</company-auth.version>
        <company-utils.version>1.8.1</company-utils.version>
        <shared-config.version>3.2.0</shared-config.version>
        <maven-enforcer-plugin.version>3.5.0</maven-enforcer-plugin.version>
        <maven-dependency-plugin.version>3.8.1</maven-dependency-plugin.version>
        <cyclonedx-maven-plugin.version>2.8.2</cyclonedx-maven-plugin.version>
        <ossindex-maven-plugin.version>3.2.0</ossindex-maven-plugin.version>
    </properties>

    <!--
        SECURITY: Repository configuration strategy for supply chain protection.

        1. Internal Nexus is listed FIRST so Maven resolves internal artifacts there before
           checking any public repository. This mitigates dependency confusion attacks.
        2. Internal company artifacts use a unique, company-specific groupId
           (com.yourcompany.*) that attackers cannot easily squat on public registries.
        3. All repositories enforce checksums and use HTTPS only.
        4. Snapshot repositories are disabled on release repos and vice versa to prevent
           version confusion.
        5. IDEAL: Use a repository manager (Nexus/Artifactory) as a single proxy so that
           only ONE repository entry is needed here, and all routing/filtering is managed
           server-side. The configuration below shows the explicit multi-repo approach
           with security controls for environments where a single proxy isn't available.
    -->
    <repositories>
        <!-- SECURITY: Internal Nexus listed FIRST for resolution priority.
             This ensures internal groupIds (com.yourcompany.*) resolve from the
             trusted internal repository before any public source is consulted. -->
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-releases</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Releases</name>
            <!-- SECURITY: Always use HTTPS for repository URLs to prevent
                 man-in-the-middle attacks on dependency downloads -->
            <url>https://nexus.yourcompany.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <!-- SECURITY: Require checksum verification to detect tampered artifacts -->
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
                <updatePolicy>daily</updatePolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <!-- SECURITY: Disable snapshots on release repository to prevent
                     snapshot injection attacks -->
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-snapshots</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Snapshots</name>
            <url>https://nexus.yourcompany.com/repository/maven-snapshots/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
                <updatePolicy>always</updatePolicy>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <!-- SECURITY: Maven Central with strict checksum enforcement.
             Listed AFTER internal repos so internal artifacts are never
             accidentally resolved from the public registry. -->
        <repository>
            <id>maven-central-secure</id>
            <name>Maven Central (Secured)</name>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
                <updatePolicy>daily</updatePolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <!-- SECURITY: Maven Central does not host snapshots;
                     explicitly disable to prevent spoofing -->
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <pluginRepositories>
        <!-- SECURITY: Plugin repositories also need the same protections.
             Malicious plugins are a common supply chain attack vector. -->
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>company-nexus-plugins</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Plugins</name>
            <url>https://nexus.yourcompany.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>

        <pluginRepository>
            <id>central-plugins-secure</id>
            <name>Maven Central Plugins (Secured)</name>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

    <!--
        SECURITY: Distribution management credentials should NEVER be in pom.xml.
        They belong in ~/.m2/settings.xml or CI/CD secret management.
        The IDs here reference server entries in settings.xml where credentials
        are stored securely (ideally encrypted with maven master password).
    -->
    <distributionManagement>
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-releases</id>
            <name>Company Releases</name>
            <url>https://nexus.yourcompany.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
        </repository>
        <snapshotRepository>
            <id>company-nexus-snapshots</id>
            <name>Company Snapshots</name>
            <url>https://nexus.yourcompany.com/repository/maven-snapshots/</url>
        </snapshotRepository>
    </distributionManagement>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <!--
            SECURITY: Use dependencyManagement to centrally control versions.
            This prevents transitive dependencies from pulling in unexpected versions
            and ensures consistent, auditable dependency resolution across all modules.
        -->
        <dependencies>
            <!-- Jackson BOM for consistent Jackson versions across all modules -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson</groupId>
                <artifactId>jackson-bom</artifactId>
                <version>${jackson.version}</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- ==================== Spring Boot Dependencies ==================== -->
        <!-- Version managed by spring-boot-starter-parent (pinned above) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== Hibernate ==================== -->
        <!-- SECURITY: Pinned to exact version via property -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hibernate.orm</groupId>
            <artifactId>hibernate-core</artifactId>
            <version>${hibernate.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== Jackson ==================== -->
        <!-- Versions managed by Jackson BOM imported in dependencyManagement -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-annotations</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-datatype-jsr310</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== Internal Company Libraries ==================== -->
        <!--
            SECURITY: Internal libraries use company-specific groupId (com.yourcompany.libs).
            This is critical for preventing dependency confusion attacks where an attacker
            publishes a package with the same name but higher version on a public repository.

            Using a unique, company-owned groupId that is registered/reserved ensures:
            1. Public repos won't have packages under this namespace
            2. Internal Nexus resolves these first (listed first in repositories)
            3. The enforcer plugin below can ban resolution of these groupIds from public repos

            All versions are pinned to exact releases - NEVER use LATEST or RELEASE.
        -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.yourcompany.libs</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-auth</artifactId>
            <version>${company-auth.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.yourcompany.libs</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-utils</artifactId>
            <version>${company-utils.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.yourcompany.libs</groupId>
            <artifactId>shared-config</artifactId>
            <version>${shared-config.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== Test Dependencies ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-security-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <!-- Spring Boot Maven Plugin -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <!-- SECURITY: Generate build info for traceability and audit -->
                    <additionalProperties>
                        <encoding.source>${project.build.sourceEncoding}</encoding.source>
                    </additionalProperties>
                </configuration>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>build-info</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Maven Enforcer Plugin - Critical for supply chain security.
                Enforces build-time rules that prevent common dependency attacks.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-enforcer-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-secure-dependencies</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <rules>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Ban dynamic versions entirely.
                                    LATEST, RELEASE, and version ranges like [1.0,2.0)
                                    allow attackers to inject malicious versions by publishing
                                    a higher version number to any configured repository.
                                -->
                                <banDynamicVersions>
                                    <allowSnapshots>false</allowSnapshots>
                                    <allowLatest>false</allowLatest>
                                    <allowRelease>false</allowRelease>
                                    <allowRanges>false</allowRanges>
                                </banDynamicVersions>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Require all dependencies to have pinned versions.
                                    Prevents accidental use of unpinned transitive dependencies.
                                -->
                                <requireUpperBoundDeps/>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Ban known vulnerable or problematic dependencies.
                                    Maintain this list based on security advisories.
                                -->
                                <bannedDependencies>
                                    <excludes>
                                        <!-- Ban old commons-collections with known RCE vulnerabilities -->
                                        <exclude>commons-collections:commons-collections:[3.0,3.2.1]</exclude>
                                        <!-- Ban log4j-core versions affected by Log4Shell -->
                                        <exclude>org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core:[2.0,2.17.0)</exclude>
                                        <!-- Ban Spring Framework versions with known RCE -->
                                        <exclude>org.springframework:spring-core:[5.0.0,5.3.18)</exclude>
                                    </excludes>
                                    <searchTransitive>true</searchTransitive>
                                </bannedDependencies>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Require minimum Maven version for security fixes -->
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.9.0,)</version>
                                    <message>Maven 3.9.0+ required for improved dependency resolution security.</message>
                                </requireMavenVersion>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Require minimum Java version -->
                                <requireJavaVersion>
                                    <version>[21,)</version>
                                    <message>Java 21+ required for latest security features.</message>
                                </requireJavaVersion>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Ensure HTTPS is used for all repository URLs -->
                                <requireNoRepositories>
                                    <!--
                                        In a strict corporate environment, you may want to
                                        ban repositories in pom.xml entirely and manage them
                                        only via settings.xml. Set to true if using that approach.
                                    -->
                                    <allowedRepositories>
                                        <allowedRepository>company-nexus-releases</allowedRepository>
                                        <allowedRepository>company-nexus-snapshots</allowedRepository>
                                        <allowedRepository>maven-central-secure</allowedRepository>
                                    </allowedRepositories>
                                    <allowedPluginRepositories>
                                        <allowedPluginRepository>company-nexus-plugins</allowedPluginRepository>
                                        <allowedPluginRepository>central-plugins-secure</allowedPluginRepository>
                                    </allowedPluginRepositories>
                                </requireNoRepositories>
                            </rules>
                            <fail>true</fail>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Dependency analysis to detect unused/undeclared dependencies.
                Undeclared dependencies resolved transitively can be hijacked.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-dependency-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>analyze-dependencies</id>
                        <phase>verify</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>analyze-only</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!-- Warn on undeclared transitive dependencies being used directly -->
                            <failOnWarning>false</failOnWarning>
                            <ignoreNonCompile>true</ignoreNonCompile>
                            <outputXML>true</outputXML>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Generate Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) in CycloneDX format.
                SBOMs are essential for:
                - Tracking all dependencies and their versions
                - Rapid vulnerability response (know what you're running)
                - Compliance and audit requirements
                - Supply chain transparency
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.cyclonedx</groupId>
                <artifactId>cyclonedx-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${cyclonedx-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <phase>package</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>makeAggregateBom</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
                <configuration>
                    <projectType>application</projectType>
                    <schemaVersion>1.5</schemaVersion>
                    <includeBomSerialNumber>true</includeBomSerialNumber>
                    <includeCompileScope>true</includeCompileScope>
                    <includeProvidedScope>true</includeProvidedScope>
                    <includeRuntimeScope>true</includeRuntimeScope>
                    <includeSystemScope>false</includeSystemScope>
                    <includeTestScope>false</includeTestScope>
                    <includeLicenseText>false</includeLicenseText>
                    <outputFormat>json</outputFormat>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: OSS Index vulnerability scanning.
                Checks all dependencies against known vulnerability databases
                during the build process. Fails the build if vulnerabilities
                above the configured CVSS threshold are found.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.sonatype.ossindex.maven</groupId>
                <artifactId>ossindex-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${ossindex-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>audit-dependencies</id>
                        <phase>validate</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>audit</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
                <configuration>
                    <!-- Fail build on vulnerabilities with CVSS score >= 7.0 (High/Critical) -->
                    <cvssScoreThreshold>7.0</cvssScoreThreshold>
                    <reportFile>${project.build.directory}/ossindex-audit-report.json</reportFile>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Compiler plugin with secure defaults -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <source>${java.version}</source>
                    <target>${java.version}</target>
                    <parameters>true</parameters>
                    <!-- SECURITY: Enable all warnings to catch potential issues early -->
                    <showWarnings>true</showWarnings>
                    <showDeprecation>true</showDeprecation>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- SECURITY: Reproducible builds - ensures build output is deterministic -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-jar-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <archive>
                        <manifest>
                            <addDefaultImplementationEntries>true</addDefaultImplementationEntries>
                            <addBuildEnvironmentEntries>true</addBuildEnvironmentEntries>
                        </manifest>
                        <manifestEntries>
                            <Build-Timestamp>${maven.build.timestamp}</Build-Timestamp>
                        </manifestEntries>
                    </archive>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <!--
            SECURITY: Dedicated profile for comprehensive security scanning in CI/CD.
            Run with: mvn verify -Psecurity-scan
        -->
        <profile>
            <id>security-scan</id>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.sonatype.ossindex.maven</groupId>
                        <artifactId>ossindex-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                        <version>${ossindex-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <id>full-audit</id>
                                <phase>validate</phase>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>audit</goal>
                                </goals>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                        <configuration>
                            <!-- In security scan profile, fail on any known vulnerability -->
                            <cvssScoreThreshold>0</cvssScoreThreshold>
                            <reportFile>${project.build.directory}/security-audit-report.json</reportFile>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>
</project>
